mnemonics: An Entity Set is like a Noun (e.g., 'Student') and its Attributes are Adjectives (e.g., 'student_id', 'name'). A Relationship Set is the Verb connecting the Nouns (e.g., 'Enrolls_In').
keyPoints: An entity set is a collection of similar entities (e.g., all 'Students').
Structural constraints define the rules for relationship participation: cardinality ratio (1:1, 1:N, M:N) and participation constraint (total/partial).
Constraints are not just guidelines; they are enforced rules to maintain data integrity and model real-world rules accurately.
commonMistakes: Confusing entity sets (the blueprint/table) with entities (a single instance/row).
Mixing up cardinality (e.g., 1:N) with participation (mandatory vs. optional).
Forgetting that 'many' (N or M) in cardinality means 'zero or more', not 'one or more'.
